description.para: Popupmenu indizes for the PA plot controls

briefdescription.para: Return a list of all graphs.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, variable displayMode, variable channelNumber, variable activeRegionCount)
memberdef.name: PA_GetGraphName

briefdescription.para: Return the subwindow path to the panel which holds the graphs with the color scales.

detaileddescription.para: Only present for PA_DISPLAYMODE_IMAGES graphs.

briefdescription.para: Return the subwindow path to the graph which holds the color scales.

detaileddescription.para: Only present for PA_DISPLAYMODE_IMAGES graphs.

memberdef.argsstring: (string mainWin, PulseAverageSettings *pa, variable displayMode, variable channelNumber, variable region, variable activeRegionCount, variable activeChanCount, variable numRegions)
memberdef.name: PA_GetGraph

briefdescription.para: Return the name of the pulse average graph.

detaileddescription.para: This function takes care of creating a graph if it does not exist, and laying it out correctly

Layout scheme for multiple graphs turned on:
Positions the graphs right to mainWin in matrix form
Columns: Regions (aka headstages with pulse starting time information respecting region selection in GUI)
Rows: Active unique channels

briefdescription.para: Return the names of the vertical and horizontal axes.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave DA, string fullPath, variable channelNumber, variable totalOnsetDelay)
memberdef.name: PA_CalculatePulseInfos

briefdescription.para: Derive the pulse infos from a DA wave.

detaileddescription.para: Uses plain FindLevels after the onset delay using 10% of the full range above the minimum as threshold

simplesect.para: pulse info wave or if nothing could be found, an invalid wave reference

briefdescription.para: Return a wave with headstage numbers, duplicates replaced with NaN so that the indizes still correspond the ones in traceData.

memberdef.argsstring: (WaveText traceData, variable idx, variable region, string channelTypeStr)
memberdef.name: PA_GetPulseInfos

briefdescription.para: Return pulse infos.

parameterdescription.para: 2D wave with trace information, from GetTraceInfos()
Index into traceData, used for determining sweep numbers, labnotebooks, etc.
Region (headstage) to get pulse starting times for
Type of the channel, one of XOP_CHANNEL_NAMES

simplesect.para: invalid wave reference if no pulses could be found or 2D wave see GetPulseInfoWave()

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, WaveOrNull pulseInfosEpochs, WaveOrNull pulseInfosCalc)
memberdef.name: PA_DiffPulseInfos

briefdescription.para: Compare epoch and calculated pulse infos.
Extracts the pulse info from the lab notebook and returns them as wave.

parameterdescription.para: epoch data to extract pulse starting times

simplesect.para: pulse info, see GetPulseInfoWave() or an invalid wave reference on error

memberdef.argsstring: (wave pulseInfos, variable pulseIndex, variable overridePulseLength, variable fixedPulseLength)
memberdef.name: PA_GetPulseLength

memberdef.argsstring: (WaveOrNull wv, dfref singleSweepFolder, variable channelType, variable channelNumber, variable clampMode, variable region, variable pulseIndex, variable first, variable length, wave pulseInfos)
memberdef.name: PA_CreateAndFillPulseWaveIfReq

briefdescription.para: Single pulse wave creator.

detaileddescription.para: The wave note for the pulse waves is stored in a separate empty wave. This speeds up the caching logic for the pulse waves a lot.

The wave note is used for documenting the applied operations:
$NOTE_KEY_FAILED_PULSE_LEVEL: Level used for failed pulse search
$NOTE_KEY_NUMBER_OF_SPIKES: Number of spikes used for failed pulse search
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_LENGTH: Length in points of the pulse wave (before any operations)
$NOTE_KEY_SEARCH_FAILED_PULSE: Checkbox state of "Search failed pulses"
$NOTE_KEY_TIMEALIGN: Time alignment was active and applied
$NOTE_KEY_TIMEALIGN_TOTAL_OFFSET: Calculated offset from time alignment
$NOTE_KEY_ZEROED: Zeroing was active and applied
$NOTE_KEY_WAVE_MINIMUM: Minimum value of the data
$NOTE_KEY_WAVE_MAXIMUM: Maximum value of the data
$NOTE_KEY_TIMEALIGN_FEATURE_POS: Position where the feature for time alignment was found
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_IS_DIAGONAL: Stores if pulse is shown in the diagonal of the output layout
$PA_SOURCE_WAVE_TIMESTAMP: Last modification time of the pulse wave before creation.
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_START: ms coordinates where the pulse starts
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_END: ms coordinates where the pulse ends
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_CLAMPMODE: Clamp mode of the pulse data, one of AmplifierClampModes

Diagonal pulses only with failed pulse search enabled:
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_HAS_FAILED: Pulse has failed
$NOTE_KEY_PULSE_SPIKE_POSITIONS: Comma separated list of spike positions in ms. 0 is the start of the pulse.

briefdescription.para: Generate a key for a pulse.

detaileddescription.para: All pulses with that key are either failing or passing.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, variable mode, WaveOrNull additionalData)
memberdef.name: PA_GenerateAllPulseWaves

briefdescription.para: Create all single pulse waves.

detaileddescription.para: This function needs to be called when ever traces in the databrowser/sweepbrowser are removed or added.

Idea:
Gather all AD sweep traces in the databrowser/sweepbrowser (skipping duplicates from oodDAQ)
Iterate over all regions (there are as many regions as unique headstages)
Now gather the pulse starting time from the region and create single pulse waves for all of them
The result is feed into GetPulseAverageProperties() and GetPulseAveragepropertiesWaves() for further consumption.

Fast path for incremental update:
The previous list of regions/channels is saved
We get the indizes of the new sweep (from additionalData)
Only these indizes are added to the properties wave, as well as the setIndice waves at the end
information is stored where the new data begins
The region/channels of the new Sweep(s) are merged with the old ones.
In case the layout changed compared to the old regions/channels it is calculated again.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave indices, variable startIndex, variable numEntries, WaveRefWave propertiesWaves, variable isDiagonal)
memberdef.name: PA_SetDiagonalityNote

briefdescription.para: This function fills a structure with information we need in most further processing functions, e.g. PA DFs, references to properties, setIndice waves, regions, channels, axes, a.s.o. Since not every information might be available at the time this structure is filled there are two steps defined, that are enabled by setting 'part' with PA_PASIINIT_BASE and/or PA_PASIINIT_INDICEMETA.

parameterdescription.para: PulseAverageSettings structure
filling step for the returned structure, set depending on available data
When set then the meta information for incremental updates is just initialized with zero.

briefdescription.para: For incremental display update copy current size of of setIndices to new display start.

memberdef.definition: static std::tuple< WaveRefWave, WAVE, WAVE, WAVE > PA_GetSetIndicesHelper
memberdef.name: PA_GetSetIndicesHelper

briefdescription.para: Retrieve setIndices, channels and regions of the current or the previous layout, as is saved in the properties wave wave note.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to pulse average helper DF

When set then the indices, channels and regions of the previous layout are returned, otherwise the current layout is returned. Note that only the layout is considered, the returned indices always contain the current indices for each channel/region at the time of calling this function.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave currentDisplayMapping, wave prevDisplayMapping, PulseAverageSetIndices *pasi, variable layoutChanged)
memberdef.name: PA_UpdateIndiceNotes

briefdescription.para: Updates the setIndices notes with information about the layout and layout changes. Therefore the function compares the previous display mapping and the current display mapping for each region/channel in the layout and determines if the setIndices at this region/channel are added as new/moved/stayed at the same position or were removed. In the setIndices wave note the following keys are set: '$PA_SETINDICES_KEY_DISPCHANGE': change in the layout for this set '$PA_SETINDICES_KEY_ACTIVEREGIONCOUNT': grid location on the vertical where this set is displayed '$PA_SETINDICES_KEY_ACTIVECHANCOUNT': grid location on the horizontal where this set is displayed (this data is currently not used, but created for further PA plot extension)

parameterdescription.para: 3D wave, rows and columns map the region/channels, in the two layers the associated activeRegion and activeChannel are stored. The layer information is the position in the grid of the layout.
Technically the same wave as currentDisplayMapping, but it stores the information about the previous layout.
Pulse Average structure storing PA information
when set then the layout has changed compared to the previous one displayed. This is directly related to a region/channel change.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave indices, wave currentMap, wave oldMap, variable channel, variable region, variable layoutChanged, variable indiceType)
memberdef.name: PA_UpdateIndiceNotesImpl

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the previous and current mapping and set the display change in the wave note of the indice sets as well as activeChanCount, activeRegionCount. IMPORTANT: To have a consistent state for the case the layout changed the function must be called with the current and the previous indices. Otherwise removed sets wont be flagged properly.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave setIndices, variable channelNumber, variable region, wave properties, PulseAverageSettings *pa)
memberdef.name: PA_ApplyPulseSortingOrder

briefdescription.para: Populates pps.pulseAverSett with the user selection from the panel.
gather deconvolution settings from PA section in BSP
Update the PA plot to accomodate changed settings.

memberdef.argsstring: (dfref dfr, variable channelNumber, variable region, variable removeFailedPulses=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: PA_GetSetWaves

briefdescription.para: Returns the two column setWave with pulse/pulsenote.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave properties, WaveRefWave propertiesWaves, wave setIndizes, variable getMode, variable removeFailedPulses)
memberdef.name: PA_GetSetWaves_TS

briefdescription.para: Returns a 1D wave ref wave containing the refs to the setwave2 refs of {all, new, old} sets, depending on the mode constant given in getModes. For mode constants.

simplesect.para: PAGetSetWavesModes, they can be combined by ORing the bits. Each setWave2 component wave entry in the returned wave is a 2D wave ref wave that refrences the pulse data in col 0, and the pulse note in col 1. The rows count the pulses.

briefdescription.para: Handle marking pulses as failed/passed if required.
This function returns data from the light-weight data storage for PA graph data.

parameterdescription.para: name of PA graph
[optional, default = 0] when set reinitializes the data for the given graph

simplesect.para: row index of the wave where the graph data is stored

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, PA_ConstantSettings *cs, PulseAverageSetIndices *pasi, variable mode)
memberdef.name: PA_ShowPulses

briefdescription.para: Remove all traces, image and annotations from the graph and clears its trace user data.
Returns a filled structure PA_ConstantSettings which has 1 for all constant entries of the given category.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, PA_ConstantSettings *cs, variable mode, WaveOrNull additionalData)
memberdef.name: PA_PreProcessPulses

briefdescription.para: Gather and pre-process the single pulses for display.

detaileddescription.para: This function is display-type agnostic and only does preparational steps. No graphs are created or killed.

The work with pulses is done in the following order:
Gather pulses
Sort pulses (in setIndizes)
Reset pulses to backup
Failed pulse marking
Zeroing
Time alignment
Averaging

parameterdescription.para: structure keeping references to current PA data set
dest boolean denoting if there are pulses to plot

memberdef.argsstring: (dfref dfr, WaveOrNull avg, variable channel, variable region)
memberdef.name: PA_MakeAverageWavePermanent

briefdescription.para: Stores the WaveMaximum in the wave note of the given wave and sets the wave unit to the same as from unitSource.

parameterdescription.para: Wave where the maximum is determined and written to the wave note, the wave unit determiend from unitSource is also set for w
a source wave for wave unit information for w

briefdescription.para: Update the scale bars of the passed plot.

detaileddescription.para:
win
PA trace or image plot
resetToUserLength
Reset the scale bars to the user upplied values
This functions is non-statc as it is called from the operation queue. This is necessary as the window hooks listen to the mouse wheel event, and we want to update only after the mouse wheel triggered the axis range change. And that is only possible with the operation queue.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, PulseAverageSetIndices *pasi, variable displayMode, variable axisMode, variable resetToUserLength=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: PA_DrawScaleBars

memberdef.definition: static std::tuple< variable, variable, variable, variable > PA_GetMinAndMax
memberdef.name: PA_GetMinAndMax

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, string userDataName, variable physicalLength, variable axisMinimum, variable axisMaximum, variable userLength=defaultValue, string userLengthName=defaultValue, variable resetToUserLength=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: PA_NeedsForcedScaleBar

briefdescription.para: Determine if we need a scale bar label.

detaileddescription.para: Without any optional parameters we always need a label if we have a stored entry userDataName and that differs from physicalLength.

With the optional parameters we prioritize userLength if it has changed compared to the stored value userLengthName or if it needs resetting on a new plot. Otherwise we use the same approach as above.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, variable axisMode, variable displayMode, WaveRefWave setWaves2, string vertAxis, string horizAxis, variable ylength, string xUnit, string yUnit, variable activeChanCount, variable activeRegionCount, variable numActive, variable resetToUserLength)
memberdef.name: PA_DrawScaleBarsHelper

briefdescription.para: Calculate the number of spikes in the given wave for IC and I=0 data.

memberdef.argsstring: (variable channelType, variable channelNumber, variable region, variable pulseIndex)
memberdef.name: PA_GeneratePulseWaveName

briefdescription.para: Generate the wave name for a single pulse.
Generate a static base name for objects in the current averaging folder.
Zero single pulses using ZeroWave.
Zero the wave using differentiation and integration.

detaileddescription.para: Overwrites the input wave Preserves the WaveNote and adds the entry NOTE_KEY_ZEROED
2D waves are zeroed along each row

memberdef.argsstring: (wave average, dfref outputDFR, string outputWaveName, PulseAverageDeconvSettings *deconvolution)
memberdef.name: PA_Deconvolution

briefdescription.para: Time alignment for PA single pulses.

para.verbatim: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * See :ref:`db_paplot_timealignment` for an explanation of the algorithm. *

briefdescription.para: Reset All pulse and pulse note waves from a set to its original state if they are outdated.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, string colorScaleGraph, string name, string text, variable index, variable numSlots, string traceName)
memberdef.name: PA_AddColorScale

briefdescription.para: Write the PA settings pa to the panel user data and return a JSON id with the settings.

simplesect.para: Valid JSON id, caller must release memory.

briefdescription.para: Read the PA settings from the panel user data into pa and return a JSON id with the settings.

simplesect.para: Valid JSON id, caller must release memory, or NaN on error/incompatible struct

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, PulseAverageSettings *pa, PA_ConstantSettings *cs, PulseAverageSetIndices *pasi, variable mode, WaveOrNull additionalData)

briefdescription.para: Apply the given color scale to all PA plot images.
Adjust the size of the panel with the color scale graph.

memberdef.argsstring: (PulseAverageSettings *pa, PulseAverageSetIndices *pasi, variable xLoc, variable yLoc, variable displayMode)
memberdef.name: PA_IsDataOnSubPlot

briefdescription.para: Returns 1 if data is displayed on the given layout grid location. When data is displayed in PA_ShowPulses and PA_ShowImage then in PulseAverageSetIndices this is tagged.

parameterdescription.para: Pulse Average Setting information
Pulse Average Set Indices information
x location on display grid, typically the channel index
y location on display grid, typically the region index
Return data for either trace plot (PA_DISPLAYMODE_TRACES) or image plot (PA_DISPLAYMODE_IMAGES)

simplesect.para: 1 if data is displayed on this grid location, 0 if no data is displayed

briefdescription.para: Runs through all graph groups in the json and appends them to the graph.

memberdef.argsstring: (string w, string v, string h, variable r, variable g, variable b, variable a, variable s, wave y, WaveText t, WaveRefWave d)
memberdef.name: PA_AccelerateAppendTracesImpl

briefdescription.para: Appends a group of traces to a graph, properties v to s must be constant for the group.

parameterdescription.para: name of graph window
name of vertical axis
name of horizontal axis
red color component
green color component
blue color component
alpha component
step width of graph display
1D wave with indices into wave d for the actual plot data
1D wave with trace names, same size as y
wave reference wave with plot data

briefdescription.para: PA Routines for dealing with pulse averaging.

listitem.para: Averaging is done for all pulses in a set
Zeroing is done for all pulses
Deconvolution is done for the average wave only

detaileddescription.para:
Drawing layers:
ProgAxes: X=0 line for images
ProgFront: Scale bars
ProgBack: Failed pulses triangles
